findings.message: `"command": "npx", "args": ["-y", "@ycse/nanobanana-mcp"]` auto-installs and executes an npm package every time the MCP server starts. The `-y` flag suppresses the confirmation prompt. A typosquatted or compromised `@ycse/nanobanana-mcp` package would silently execute in Claude Code's process context.

Documented install method is `curl -sL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/AgriciDaniel/claude-blog/main/install.sh | bash`. An attacker who compromises the GitHub repository or performs MITM on raw.githubusercontent.com can deliver and execute arbitrary code on the installer's machine without any signature verification.

CLAUDE.md states 14 reference files in the architecture overview but lists only 13 by name. `skills/blog-write/SKILL.md` references `references/cta-placement.md` which is not in the named list — likely the 14th file — creating an inconsistency between the stated count and the documented inventory.
No model declared in frontmatter; agent will inherit caller's default rather than an intentional model choice.
Zero example blocks in agent definition; Claude has no concrete input/output demonstrations to calibrate behavior.
Five vague quantifiers found: "suitable" (×2), "appropriate" (×2), "relevant" (×1). Each instance removes precision from agent instructions. Penalty capped at −8.
No model declared in frontmatter; agent will inherit caller's default rather than an intentional model choice.
Zero example blocks in agent definition; Claude has no concrete input/output demonstrations to calibrate behavior.
`Bash` declared in `tools` but never invoked in the workflow; the agent only reads files and produces a scoring report. Unused tool declarations expand the agent's attack surface without benefit.
No model declared in frontmatter; agent will inherit caller's default rather than an intentional model choice.
Zero example blocks in agent definition; Claude has no concrete input/output demonstrations to calibrate behavior.
No model declared in frontmatter; agent will inherit caller's default rather than an intentional model choice.
Zero example blocks in agent definition; Claude has no concrete input/output demonstrations to calibrate behavior.
Two vague quantifiers found: "natural" (×1), "relevant" (×1). Penalty −4.

`pip3 install --quiet -r requirements.txt` is called without `--require-hashes`. `requirements.txt` uses range pins (`>=0.7.3,<1.0.0`) but no hash verification. A dependency-confusion or typosquatting attack against `textstat` or `beautifulsoup4` on PyPI could substitute a malicious build at install time.
Two vague quantifiers found: "appropriate" (×1), "relevant" (×1). Penalty −4.

`script_path = skill_dir / "scripts" / script_name` where `script_name` is taken directly from `sys.argv[1]` without sanitization. Python's pathlib does not strip `../` sequences; a crafted argument such as `../../../../../../bin/python` resolves outside the scripts directory. The same pattern appears in skills/blog-audio/scripts/run.py:73 and skills/blog-google/scripts/run.py:73.
Three vague quantifiers found: "appropriate" (×1), "relevant" (×2). Penalty −6.
Four vague quantifiers found: "appropriate" (×1), "relevant" (×2), "authentic" (×1). Penalty −8.
Six vague quantifiers found: "appropriate" (×2), "relevant" (×3), "suitable" (×1). Penalty −12.

References `references/cta-placement.md` at lines 161 and 165, but this file is not listed in CLAUDE.md's architecture section (which enumerates 13 of the stated 14 reference files). If the file is absent from the installed skill directory, the writer agent silently misses CTA placement guidance.
No model declared in frontmatter; agent will inherit caller's default rather than an intentional model choice.

Documented install method is `curl -sL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/AgriciDaniel/claude-blog/main/install.sh | bash`. An attacker who compromises the GitHub repository or performs MITM on raw.githubusercontent.com can deliver and execute arbitrary code on the installer's machine without any signature verification.

`"command": "npx", "args": ["-y", "@ycse/nanobanana-mcp"]` auto-installs and executes an npm package every time the MCP server starts. The `-y` flag suppresses the confirmation prompt. A typosquatted or compromised `@ycse/nanobanana-mcp` package would silently execute in Claude Code's process context.

`script_path = skill_dir / "scripts" / script_name` where `script_name` is taken directly from `sys.argv[1]` without sanitization. Python's pathlib does not strip `../` sequences; a crafted argument such as `../../../../../../bin/python` resolves outside the scripts directory. The same pattern appears in skills/blog-audio/scripts/run.py:73 and skills/blog-google/scripts/run.py:73.

`pip3 install --quiet -r requirements.txt` is called without `--require-hashes`. `requirements.txt` uses range pins (`>=0.7.3,<1.0.0`) but no hash verification. A dependency-confusion or typosquatting attack against `textstat` or `beautifulsoup4` on PyPI could substitute a malicious build at install time.

References `references/cta-placement.md` at lines 161 and 165, but this file is not listed in CLAUDE.md's architecture section (which enumerates 13 of the stated 14 reference files). If the file is absent from the installed skill directory, the writer agent silently misses CTA placement guidance.

CLAUDE.md states 14 reference files in the architecture overview but lists only 13 by name. `skills/blog-write/SKILL.md` references `references/cta-placement.md` which is not in the named list — likely the 14th file — creating an inconsistency between the stated count and the documented inventory.
